strengths: 15+ years of polyglot engineering across web, mobile, embedded and high-throughput distributed systems, including micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic — directly relevant to the CTO role's distributed-systems and real-time inference demands.

Current Staff Software Engineer role at re:cinq explicitly contributing to AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks — hands-on agentic experience aligned with the role's LangGraph/custom orchestration needs.

Founder of GISCollective, an event-driven open-source platform deployed on Kubernetes, demonstrating end-to-end architectural ownership, event-driven design and long-term product leadership relevant to building a modular multi-agent framework.
concerns: Resume title is Senior/Staff Software Engineer rather than VP/CTO, and there is no explicit evidence of scaling an engineering org from 10 to 30+ or building a senior leadership layer.
Fintech exposure is limited to RIDE Capital (investment platform, ~15 months) without explicit credit-risk, working-capital or regulated multi-jurisdiction compliance experience called out.
No stated experience governing outsourced/hybrid delivery partners or operating multi-agent AI systems specifically in regulated, high-stakes commercial production settings.

key_factors.factor: Hands-on agentic AI and MCP infrastructure work at re:cinq
Distributed systems depth (event-driven, low-latency, high-QPS micro-services)
Fintech-adjacent experience at RIDE Capital with craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based)

response.justification: The candidate offers a rare combination of current, hands-on agentic-AI work (MCP generators and agentic frameworks at re:cinq) layered on top of 15+ years of distributed-systems craftsmanship — including event-driven, low-latency micro-services at Sociomantic scale and a self-built, Kubernetes-deployed event-driven platform at GISCollective. Fintech exposure at RIDE Capital, paired with a disciplined engineering culture (TDD, pairing, trunk-based development), maps directly to the role's demand for production reliability, evaluation rigor and auditable agent behaviour, making this a defensible upside bet for a technically-deep, agentic-AI-native CTO.
